answer:
Sketch:
- LTV:CAC ~2.2x — below 3x benchmark but not broken
- Payback: $900 / $150 = 6 months — acceptable for B2B SaaS
- $6k/mo ≈ 6–7 customers/mo — small sample, noisy CAC
- Levers: search terms/negatives, landing page CVR, branded vs non-branded split

Answer: Fix it — don't kill it. A 2.2x LTV:CAC with 6-month payback is workable, just thin. Before adding budget, do three things: (1) split branded vs. non-branded spend — branded often flatters blended CAC; judge non-branded on its own; (2) prune search terms and add negatives — at $6k/mo, 20–30% of spend is usually waste; (3) attack landing page conversion, since a 30% CVR lift drops CAC to ~$700 and gets you to ~3x. Also sanity-check the $2k LTV — at $150/mo that implies ~13-month retention; if churn improves, the channel improves for free. Kill it only if non-branded CAC alone exceeds ~$1,300–1,400 after a month of cleanup.
